content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks for code and general LLM output refinement can be augmented by incorporating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives at the core of recent LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-perspective self-refinement pipeline that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no additional training or supervised data while further improving output quality. This hybrid framework addresses a key unspoken limitation of vanilla Self-Refine: its reliance on a single perspective for self-feedback that can get stuck in local optima of output quality.
